acceptance_criteria: A contract test boots sim-core from a compiled content-runtime M2 content pack through an explicit boot DTO/schema boundary.
The test proves sim-core does not import content-source files, Node filesystem IO, React, PixiJS, Electron, DOM, or real-time APIs.
Valid M2 content boots deterministically in Node runner or in-process test host and produces a stable initial state hash.
Invalid or schema-mismatched content-runtime packs are rejected with structured errors before WorldState mutation.
This explicitly closes M1-GATE-FOLLOW-UP-002 during M2 world/content integration.
No economy, population, route-choice, UI, platform-host hash, or product-scope implementation is added beyond the boot boundary contract.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-BOOT-BOUNDARY-001
notes: Keep sim-core as the functional core and content-runtime as a validated read-only pack boundary.
If this requires a new public boot contract, systems_architect review is mandatory before implementation is considered accepted.
